Scared › Weak › Worthless.feel: Heavy, hollow, or invisible — as if nothing you do carries weight or matters.
Scared › Weak › Worthless.body: Low energy, slumped posture, tight chest or throat, shallow breathing, or feeling numb and disconnected.
Scared › Weak › Worthless.call: A call to reconnect with your inherent worth — not performance — and to seek safe support or professional care when shame runs deep.
Scared › Weak › Worthless.overcome: Name one small true thing you did today; limit doom-scrolling comparisons; move gently (walk, stretch); reach out to one trusted person; consider counseling if worthlessness feels constant.

Scared › Weak › Insignificant.feel: Overlooked, small, or forgettable — like your presence barely registers.
Scared › Weak › Insignificant.body: Tension in jaw or shoulders, restless legs, stomach butterflies, or shutting down and going quiet.
Scared › Weak › Insignificant.call: A call to notice where you are valued (even quietly) and to practice speaking needs without apologizing for existing.
Scared › Weak › Insignificant.overcome: Write three facts that contradict "I don't matter"; join one activity where contribution is visible; set one boundary; celebrate micro-wins aloud.

Scared › Weak › Inadequate.feel: Never quite enough — behind, under-skilled, or one mistake away from failure.
Scared › Weak › Inadequate.body: Racing thoughts, difficulty sleeping, clenched hands, fatigue after hyper-focus, or avoiding tasks entirely.
Scared › Weak › Inadequate.call: A call to separate identity from output — adequacy as growth and honesty rather than flawless performance.
Scared › Weak › Inadequate.overcome: Break tasks into absurdly small steps; ask "good enough for today"; share unfinished work with one safe colleague or friend; challenge all-or-nothing thoughts on paper.
